196: pub fn get_all_pr_statuses_in_repo(repo: Option<&str>) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, PrInfo> {
197:     let mut map = std::collections::HashMap::new();
198:     let mut page = 1;
199: 
200:     loop {
201:         let route = match repo {
202:             Some(r) => format!("repos/{r}/pulls?state=all&per_page=100&page={page}"),
203:             None => format!("repos/{{owner}}/{{repo}}/pulls?state=all&per_page=100&page={page}"),
204:         };
205:         let output = run_gh(&["api", &route]);
206: 
207:         let Ok(json_str) = output else {
208:             break;
209:         };
210:         let Ok(values) = serde_json::from_str::<Vec<serde_json::Value>>(&json_str) else {
211:             break;
212:         };
213:         if values.is_empty() {
214:             break;
215:         }
216: 
217:         merge_pr_status_page(&mut map, &values);
218: 
219:         if values.len() < 100 {
220:             break;
221:         }
222:         page += 1;
223:     }
224: 
225:     map
226: }
227: 
228: fn merge_pr_status_page(
229:     map: &mut std::collections::HashMap<String, PrInfo>,
230:     values: &[serde_json::Value],
231: ) {
232:     for value in values {
233:         let Some((head, pr)) = pr_info_from_rest_value(value) else {
234:             continue;
235:         };
236:         // Keep the first PR we see for a branch name. The REST API returns newest
237:         // PRs first, so later pages may contain stale historical PRs for reused names.
238:         map.entry(head).or_insert(pr);
239:     }
240: }
241: 
242: fn pr_info_from_rest_value(value: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<(String, PrInfo)> {
243:     let head = value["head"]["ref"].as_str()?.to_string();
244:     Some((
245:         head,
246:         PrInfo {
247:             number: value["number"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0),
248:             url: value["html_url"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
249:             state: value["state"]
250:                 .as_str()
251:                 .unwrap_or("UNKNOWN")
252:                 .to_ascii_uppercase(),
253:             title: value["title"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
254:             base: value["base"]["ref"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string(),
255:             is_draft: value["draft"].as_bool().unwrap_or(false),
256:             merged: !value["merged_at"].is_null(),
257:         },
258:     ))
259: }

263: }
